title: Doctorate of theology

infobox.value: ([Latin](/wiki/Latin_language "Latin language"): *Doctor Theologiae*, abbreviated **DTh**, **ThD**, **DTheol**, or **Dr. theol.**) is a [terminal degree](/wiki/Terminal_degree "Terminal degree") in the academic discipline of [theology](/wiki/Theology "Theology"). The ThD, like the ecclesiastical [Doctor of Sacred Theology](/wiki/Doctor_of_Sacred_Theology "Doctor of Sacred Theology"), is an advanced research degree equivalent to the [Doctor of Philosophy](/wiki/Doctor_of_Philosophy "Doctor of Philosophy").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Doctorate of theology](/w/index.php?title=Doctorate_of_theology&redirect=no "Doctorate of theology"))

Doctoral degree

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0f/Desmond_Tutu_Honorary_Doctorate_Vienna.jpg/250px-Desmond_Tutu_Honorary_Doctorate_Vienna.jpg)](/wiki/File:Desmond_Tutu_Honorary_Doctorate_Vienna.jpg)

Conferral of a Doctorate of Theology *[honoris causa](/wiki/Honoris_causa "Honoris causa")* on [Desmond Tutu](/wiki/Desmond_Tutu "Desmond Tutu") (right) by the [University of Vienna](/wiki/University_of_Vienna "University of Vienna").

**Doctor of Theology** ([Latin](/wiki/Latin_language "Latin language"): *Doctor Theologiae*, abbreviated **DTh**, **ThD**, **DTheol**, or **Dr. theol.**) is a [terminal degree](/wiki/Terminal_degree "Terminal degree") in the academic discipline of [theology](/wiki/Theology "Theology"). The ThD, like the ecclesiastical [Doctor of Sacred Theology](/wiki/Doctor_of_Sacred_Theology "Doctor of Sacred Theology"), is an advanced research degree equivalent to the [Doctor of Philosophy](/wiki/Doctor_of_Philosophy "Doctor of Philosophy").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Terminology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Doctor_of_Theology&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Terminology")]

In the academic study of theology, often deeply rooted in the Christian religion, the nomenclature of doctoral degrees varies between Doctor of Theology, [Doctor of Philosophy](/wiki/Doctor_of_Philosophy "Doctor of Philosophy"), and [Doctor of Sacred Theology](/wiki/Doctor_of_Sacred_Theology "Doctor of Sacred Theology"). However, [Doctor of Ministry](/wiki/Doctor_of_Ministry "Doctor of Ministry") is generally understood as a [professional doctorate](/wiki/Professional_doctorate "Professional doctorate"), whereas [Doctor of Divinity](/wiki/Doctor_of_Divinity "Doctor of Divinity") is an academic doctorate in the UK and an honorary doctorate in the USA where it is often awarded as [*honoris causa*](/wiki/Honorary_degree "Honorary degree").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### United States

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Doctor_of_Theology&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: United States")]

In the United States, some of the older theological seminaries began offering the ThD as an equivalent to the research [Doctor of Philosophy](/wiki/Doctor_of_Philosophy "Doctor of Philosophy"). In [Princeton Theological Seminary](/wiki/Princeton_Theological_Seminary "Princeton Theological Seminary"), for example, this practice was inherited from the German system of education, since the professors involved in establishing the doctoral program were trained in German universities. Though the PhD was the doctorate of arts faculties in German universities (called the philosophy faculty in Germany, hence the Ph), Princeton Theological Seminary has since switched to using Doctor of Philosophy as its main designation.[[3]](#cite_note-3) A similar shift happened at [Harvard Divinity School](/wiki/Harvard_Divinity_School "Harvard Divinity School") in 2015.[[4]](#cite_note-4) As of 2024, five [Association of Theological Schools](/wiki/Association_of_Theological_Schools "Association of Theological Schools") accredited schools currently accept students to ThD programs: [Duke Divinity School](/wiki/Duke_Divinity_School "Duke Divinity School"), [Kairos University](/wiki/Kairos_University "Kairos University") ([Evangelical Seminary](/wiki/Evangelical_Seminary "Evangelical Seminary")), [Northwest Nazarene University](/wiki/Northwest_Nazarene_University "Northwest Nazarene University")'s Graduate School of Theology, [Seventh-day Adventist Theological Seminary](/wiki/Seventh-day_Adventist_Theological_Seminary "Seventh-day Adventist Theological Seminary"), and [United Theological Seminary](/wiki/United_Theological_Seminary "United Theological Seminary").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Due to the secular push in doctoral studies, some ThD programs have moved to other known accreditation, like the [Association for Biblical Higher Education](/wiki/Association_for_Biblical_Higher_Education "Association for Biblical Higher Education") (ABHE), [Transnational Association of Christian Colleges and Schools](/wiki/Transnational_Association_of_Christian_Colleges_and_Schools "Transnational Association of Christian Colleges and Schools") (TRACS), or The Council of Private Colleges of America (CPCA).[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

### United Kingdom

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Doctor_of_Theology&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: United Kingdom")]

In the United Kingdom, the Doctor of Theology is a relatively new academic doctoral programme.[[9]](#cite_note-9) Certain universities have begun offering it as a form of research-based professional doctorate, not unlike the [Doctor of Education](/wiki/Doctor_of_Education "Doctor of Education").[[10]](#cite_note-10) To distinguish the more practical nature of the degree, some British universities have adopted the term [Doctor of Practical Theology](/wiki/Doctor_of_Practical_Theology "Doctor of Practical Theology").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

4. **[^](#cite_ref-4)** ["Degree Programs"](https://hds.harvard.edu/academics/degree-programs). *hds.harvard.edu*. Harvard Divinity School. Retrieved 23 August 2016.
